Operator: Good afternoon. Welcome to Apple's first fiscal quarter 2023 earnings conference call.

Tim Cook -- Chief Executive Officer: Thank you. Good afternoon, everyone. We are pleased to report results for our fiscal first quarter of 2023.

Prepared Remarks

We achieved revenue of $117.2 billion, up 2% year over year, and earnings per diluted share of $1.88.

Our Services business reached an all-time revenue record of $20.8 billion, up 6% year over year. We now have over 935 million paid subscriptions across our services.

iPhone revenue was $65.8 billion. Despite supply constraints in December, we are very pleased with how the quarter came together.

We continue to invest heavily in innovation across our entire product line, including our groundbreaking M-series chips which deliver industry-leading performance per watt.

Our installed base of active devices reached an all-time high, surpassing 2 billion devices. This growth reflects our deep customer loyalty and the strength of our ecosystem.

Luca Maestri -- Chief Financial Officer: Thank you, Tim. Revenue was $117.2 billion, and we achieved our best-ever EPS of $1.88, up 4% year over year.

Gross margin was 42.9%, up 40 basis points sequentially. Products gross margin was 36.3% and Services gross margin was 70.8%.

We returned $25.8 billion to our shareholders during the quarter, including $3.7 billion in dividends and equivalents, and $19.0 billion through open market repurchases of 133 million Apple shares.

For the second fiscal quarter of 2023, we expect revenue to be similar to Q1 2023 performance, with some headwinds from foreign exchange.

Questions and Answers

Operator: Our first question comes from Katie Huberty with Morgan Stanley. Please proceed.

Katie Huberty -- Morgan Stanley -- Analyst: Thank you. Tim, can you talk about demand trends you are seeing in iPhone so far in this quarter?

Tim Cook -- Chief Executive Officer: Thanks, Katie. We had strong demand for iPhone 14 and iPhone 14 Pro models. The supply constraints in December were frustrating but we worked hard to address them quickly.

Operator: Our next question comes from Amit Daryanani with Evercore ISI.

Amit Daryanani -- Evercore ISI -- Analyst: Thanks. Can you give more color on the Services growth trajectory and where you see upside?

Luca Maestri -- Chief Financial Officer: Services had a record quarter at $20.8 billion. We see continued growth driven by the expanding installed base and increasing engagement across our platforms.

Tim Cook -- Chief Executive Officer: I would add that the ecosystem continues to be a powerful driver. The health of the App Store, Apple TV+, and Apple Music all contribute to that momentum.

Operator: Our next question comes from Shannon Cross with Credit Suisse.

Shannon Cross -- Credit Suisse -- Analyst: Can you talk about margin expectations for the rest of the year and the impact of FX?

Luca Maestri -- Chief Financial Officer: On a constant currency basis, we are seeing solid margin performance. FX continues to be a headwind but we are managing costs carefully. We expect gross margins to remain strong.

Operator: This concludes the Apple Q1 2023 earnings call. Thank you for participating.
